data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness criticism strictly beats self-consistency only when SC majority is wrong (base error > 1-1/k) AND critic recall on the dominant-wrong answer is high enough to flip the plurality after filtering; it strictly loses whenever critic precision is low enough that false flags on correct minority candidates erode a formerly correct majority below the post-filter plurality threshold.", "mechanism": "SC elects the pre-filter modal answer. Harness elects the post-filter modal answer. For harness to strictly beat SC, at least one question where SC was wrong must flip to correct (critic flags enough wrong-majority candidates to drop them below the surviving minority), AND zero questions where SC was right may flip to wrong (critic must not flag so many correct-majority candidates that a surviving wrong minority overtakes them). Define p = per-candidate base error, phi = critic recall on wrong answers, psi = critic precision (fraction of flags that are truly wrong). The strict-win regime requires: (A) p > 1 - 1/k (so the wrong answer holds plurality pre-filter), and (B) phi * count(wrong_majority) > count(wrong_majority) - count(correct_minority) (filtering drops wrong below correct), and (C) (1-psi) * count(correct_majority) < count(correct_majority) - count(wrong_minority) for every question where SC was right (false flags do not let a wrong minority overtake). Condition C is the loss mode: when psi is low, false flags on correct-majority candidates can hand the win to a surviving wrong minority. The repository data sit in the null zone: p <= 1/k so SC majorities are already correct, making condition A unsatisfied and guaranteeing net fixed-minus-broke <= 0.", "scope": {"covers": ["base error p in (1-1/k, 1.0)", "critic recall phi high enough to flip plurality", "critic precision psi high enough to avoid eroding correct majorities", "k >= 3"], "excludes": ["p <= 1-1/k where SC is already correct on vast majority of questions", "critic with psi near 0 that flags indiscriminately", "k=1 or k=2"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At p=0.55 and k=5, harness accuracy is NOT at least 5 percentage points above SC accuracy, with at least 3 questions flipping from wrong-under-SC to correct-under-harness and 0 questions flipping from right-to-wrong", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At p=0.55, psi=0.15 (low precision), k=5, harness accuracy is NOT below SC accuracy, with at least 2 questions flipping from right-under-SC to wrong-under-harness due to false flags eroding correct majorities", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At p=0.15 and k=5, harness accuracy differs from SC accuracy by more than 2 percentage points in either direction", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This is the canonical mechanism-first partition: strict improvement requires a wrong SC majority to fix, and a precise-enough critic to fix it without breaking correct ones. The loss mode (false flags erode correct majorities) is the natural counterexample to any claim that filtering is monotonically beneficial. The experiment sweeps p (via task difficulty) and psi (via critic prompting) orthogonally to isolate both the win and loss regimes."}
